investment_thesis.tactical_headwind: Short-to-medium-term macroeconomic market volatility and defensive sector rotation.

scenario_a_moderate_bullishness.regime_definition: I expect the underlying stock to rise moderately towards its fair value, but near-term upside remains capped by technical overhead resistance near the 300 level. Lower premium expenditure is required.
scenario_a_moderate_bullishness.structural_justification: By purchasing an ATM call and selling a further OTM call, the short leg directly subsidizes the initial debit cost of the long leg. This structures a defined maximum loss capped at the net premium paid and limits negative portfolio time-decay (Theta) drag.

rejected_alternatives.naked_long_call: Rejected due to high unhedged premium cost exposure and rapid tail-risk loss if the stock experiences a sideways consolidation phase.
rejected_alternatives.covered_call: Rejected because it completely limits structural upside flexibility if the asset breaks out sharply, and provides virtually zero true downside capital preservation.

scenario_b_strong_bullishness.regime_definition: I have very high long-term fundamental conviction and intend to hold the underlying common stock to collect a 5.19% dividend yield, but require absolute tail-risk capital insurance against sharp market pullbacks.
scenario_b_strong_bullishness.structural_justification: Holding the underlying stock retains full participation in upside rallies and locks in recurring dividend cash flows, while the long OTM put acts as a formal insurance contract to cap maximum absolute downside risk at a predefined floor.

rejected_alternatives.naked_long_call: Rejected because a long option contract does not capture corporate dividend distributions, resulting in substantial yield sacrifice over a multi-month timeline.
